task_completion.description: Dates are formatted uniformly across the dataset
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Birth Date and Death Date columns show a consistent format (e.g., all YYYY-MM-DD) with no messy text prefixes like 'circa', 'ca.', or 'about' remaining.

task_completion.description: Duplicate records are identified or consolidated
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dataset is either reduced to approximately 32 unique rows (consolidated), or there is a specific column/indicator explicitly flagging duplicate entries (e.g., for John Smith or Mary Smith).

task_completion.description: Logical errors in the data are flagged
task_completion.visual_evidence: A column or specific text indicators flag logical errors such as 'Death before birth' (James Brown), impossible ages > 120 (Emma Brown), or parent age errors (Alice Miller).

task_completion.description: Validation formulas are implemented in the spreadsheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet formula bar displays a formula used for data validation or error checking (e.g., an IF statement comparing dates), or a Data Validation dialog box is visible in the screenshots.

task_completion.description: Records are organized by family groups
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rows are sorted or grouped by the Last Name or Family Name column, keeping family members (e.g., all Smiths, all Browns) adjacent to each other.

task_completion.description: Conditional formatting is applied to the data
task_completion.visual_evidence: Cells have automatic background or text colors applied based on their values, such as highlighted error flags, color-coded duplicates, or alternating colors for different family groups.

task_completion.description: The processed data is saved to the correct path with the correct filename
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet application window title shows 'genealogy_cleaned.xlsx', or a file manager/terminal shows this file successfully created in '/home/ga/Documents/'.

integrity.description: The agent used a spreadsheet application GUI to perform formatting and validation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a spreadsheet application (like LibreOffice Calc) open and being interacted with, rather than just terminal windows running Python/Pandas scripts.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with spreadsheet features
integrity.visual_evidence: Menus, formatting dialogs, conditional formatting rules, or the active formula bar are visible in the screenshots, showing manual or automated GUI manipulation.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by just echoing a pre-computed file
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of spreadsheet-specific visual features (like conditional formatting colors and active formulas) that cannot be easily generated just by writing a basic CSV/text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original dataset has exactly 45 records. After consolidating duplicates (e.g., John Smith, Mary Smith variants), there should be exactly 32 unique people. Known logical errors that must be flagged include: ID 11 James Brown (Death before birth), ID 13 Emma Brown (Age > 120 years), ID 25 Alice Miller (Father only 8 at birth), and ID 45 (Same-day birth-death). Date formats should be standardized from a messy mix (YYYY-MM-DD, year-only, 'circa', 'ca.') into a single uniform format.
